 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","title":"CI: optional PR comment renderer (top findings)","description":"## Objective

Optionally render a concise Markdown summary suitable for PR comments.

## Requirements

- Input: scan JSON schema v1.
- Output:
  - grouped by file
  - severity-first ordering (error, warning, info)
  - include per finding:
    - file:line
    - rule_id
    - short reason
    - truncated/redacted command preview
    - suggestion (if available)

## Markdown UX

- Use `<details><summary>` blocks per file to keep comments compact.
- Hard cap output size:
  - top N findings overall
  - per-file top M

## Determinism

- Sorting rules are deterministic and documented.

## Acceptance criteria

- Renderer output is deterministic and test-covered.
- Renderer never prints unredacted full commands when redaction is enabled.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:52.707720678-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T22:09:47.542128698-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T22:09:47.542128698-05:00","close_reason":"Added --format markdown option for PR comment output with <details> blocks, severity badges, and truncated command previews","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:19:12.458474915-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:05.839049463-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:11.120494138-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","title":"CI: scan --git-diff integration tests","description":"## Objective

Add integration tests ensuring `dcg scan --git-diff`:

- returns deterministic results
- handles empty diffs
- handles renamed/moved files gracefully

## Logging requirements

- Tests should log the diff range and files considered.

## Test plan

- Create a temp repo with two commits.
- Modify files across commits to cover:
  - added file
  - modified file
  - renamed file
  - deleted file (should be skipped gracefully)
- Run `dcg scan --git-diff <base>..<head>` and assert:
  - deterministic ordering
  - stable JSON (if using `--json`)
  - correct exit code per `--fail-on`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `dcg scan --git-diff <range>` succeeds on:
  - empty diffs (exit 0, no findings)
  - renames/moves (scans new path, no crash)
  - deletes (skips missing paths, no crash)
- Output is deterministic across runs.
- Tests emit enough logs (diff range, candidate files, filtered files) to debug failures in CI.
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### False positive regression
Include a negative fixture in the diff-range test:

- a file where a dangerous string appears only in docs/comments and must not produce a finding

This prevents CI adoption from being derailed by noisy PR checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:18:57.803468576-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T23:40:31.751720567-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T23:40:31.751720567-05:00","close_reason":"Added integration tests for dcg scan --git-diff: empty diffs, renamed files, deleted files (skipped), deterministic ordering, mixed operations","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:19:17.551755437-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:16.363785468-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:21.594148009-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:26.844970733-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","title":"Optional: package dcg scan as a reusable GitHub Action","description":"## Objective

Package `dcg scan` into a reusable GitHub Action for easier org-scale adoption.

## Why optional

A workflow snippet is enough to start. A marketplace action is accretive once:

- the scan JSON schema is stable
- allowlisting workflow is mature
- false positives are under control

## Requirements

- Action inputs:
  - `fail_on`
  - `scan_paths` or diff mode
  - `comment_on_pr`
- Action outputs:
  - summary counts
  - artifact upload

## Acceptance criteria

- Example repo can adopt by adding a few lines.
- Action pins a specific dcg version (no surprise upgrades).
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Tests
- Validate the action packaging with an end-to-end workflow in a fixture repo:
  - ensures correct exit codes
  - ensures deterministic output
  - ensures redaction/truncation does not break parsing","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:28:09.562467338-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:30:54.27869809-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:43:33.082509684-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:43:54.301284345-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:43:59.341416862-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:44:04.378980832-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:44:09.420139301-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:44:14.458422044-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.5.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:44:19.495891555-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","title":"Additional scan extractors (package.json, compose, Terraform, GitLab CI)","description":"## Objective

Expand `dcg scan` coverage with additional *high-signal executable contexts* that frequently contain destructive commands in real repos, while keeping false positives low.

This feature is intentionally **post-MVP**: we only do it after the scan schema + core CLI are stable.

## Why this matters

Teams commonly introduce destructive commands via:

- `package.json` lifecycle scripts
- `docker-compose.yml` `command`/`entrypoint`/`healthcheck.test`
- Terraform provisioners (`local-exec` / `remote-exec`)
- GitLab CI `.gitlab-ci.yml` `script:` arrays

These are *executed contexts*, so scanning them has a high signal-to-noise ratio if extraction is done precisely.

## Guardrails (false-positive immunity)

- Only extract fields that are unquestionably executed.
- Explicitly ignore data-only fields like `description`, `env`, `variables`, `labels`, etc.
- Preserve line numbers and a stable `extractor_id`.
- Provide redaction/truncation controls for potentially sensitive commands.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each extractor has fixtures that prove boundaries (executed extracted; data ignored).
- Extractors handle common YAML/JSON features (arrays, block scalars, anchors/aliases where applicable).
- Output is deterministic and stable for golden tests.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-scan.1` (schema)
- `git_safety_guard-scan.2` (CLI + file selection)
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:45:03.500293012-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T03:23:08.250765559-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:45:11.633154629-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:45:21.353464231-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-scan.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:45:26.391276899-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-t1rx","title":"Implement Tier 1 regex heredoc detector with RegexSet","description":"# Tier 1: Fast Regex Heredoc Detection

## Objective

Implement the fast-path heredoc detector using Rust's `regex` crate with RegexSet for multi-pattern matching.

## Why RegexSet?

The `regex` crate's RegexSet allows matching against multiple patterns simultaneously with:
- Aho-Corasick algorithm for literal prefixes
- SIMD acceleration (AVX2, SSE4.2)
- Single pass through input
- O(n) guaranteed time complexity

## Patterns to Implement

```rust
static HEREDOC_PATTERNS: LazyLock<RegexSet> = LazyLock::new(|| {
    RegexSet::new([
        // Bash heredocs
        r"<<-?\s*['\"]?\w+['\"]?",   // <<EOF, <<'EOF', <<-EOF
        r"<<<",                          // Here-strings

        // Inline script flags
        r"\b(python3?|python)\s+-[ce]\s",
        r"\b(ruby|irb)\s+-e\s",
        r"\bperl\s+-[eE]\s",
        r"\bnode\s+-[ep]\s",
        r"\b(sh|bash|zsh|fish)\s+-c\s",

        // Pipe to interpreter
        r"\|\s*(python3?|ruby|perl|node|sh|bash)\b",
        r"\|\s*xargs\s",
    ]).expect("heredoc patterns compile")
});
```

## API

```rust
/// Check if command might contain heredoc/inline script
/// Returns true if ANY pattern matches (fast path triggers Tier 2)
pub fn might_contain_heredoc(cmd: &str) -> bool;

/// Get which patterns matched (for debugging/logging)
pub fn matched_heredoc_patterns(cmd: &str) -> Vec<usize>;
```

## Performance Target

- < 10 microseconds for typical commands
- Zero allocations on non-match path
- Must have ZERO false negatives (can have false positives)

## Test Cases

### Must Match (true positives)
- `python3 << 'EOF'\nprint('hi')\nEOF`
- `bash -c 'echo hello'`
- `node -e "console.log(1)"`
- `curl http://x.com | bash`
- `cat << EOF | python3`

### Must NOT Match (true negatives)
- `git status`
- `ls -la`
- `cargo build`
- `echo "hello world"`

### May Match (acceptable false positives)
- `grep -e pattern file` (has -e but not dangerous)
- `echo "<<EOF"` (string contains heredoc marker)

## Dependencies

- regex = "1.10" (add to Cargo.toml)
- Depends on: Two-tier architecture design (2ta)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `might_contain_heredoc` returns true for all “Must Match” cases.
- `might_contain_heredoc` returns false for all “Must NOT Match” cases.
- Detector adds effectively-zero overhead for non-matching commands (validate via benchmark once perf harness exists).
- Patterns are compiled once (static) and never per-invocation.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMill","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:00:00-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T01:43:47.858027873-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T01:43:47.858027873-05:00","close_reason":"Implementation complete in src/heredoc.rs. All acceptance criteria met:
- check_triggers() (equiv. to might_contain_heredoc) detects all heredoc/inline script patterns
- matched_triggers() provides debugging indices
- Uses LazyLock<RegexSet> for static compilation, SIMD-accelerated matching
- 12 unit tests passing covering all required test cases
- Additional patterns included: fish -c, xargs pipe, <<~ Ruby heredocs","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t1rx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-2ta","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:28:32.034477208-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t1rx","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:50:39.537459716-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-t8x","title":"Epic: False Positive Immunity (Execution Context)","description":"# Epic: False Positive Immunity (Execution Context)

## Why this epic exists

A destructive-command guard must be *trusted*. Repeated false positives destroy velocity and lead users to disable the guard entirely.

The core failure mode is **context blindness**: matching dangerous substrings in places where they are clearly *data*, not *executed code*.

Examples that must be allowed:

- `bd create --description="This pattern blocks rm -rf"`
- `git commit -m "Fix git reset --hard detection"`
- `echo "example: git push --force"`
- `rg -n "rm -rf" src/main.rs`

Currently, a purely regex-based guard will often match `rm -rf` (or similar) inside quoted strings and block legitimate documentation/testing work.

## Key idea

Introduce an **Execution Context layer** that classifies which parts of the command line are actually executed vs merely data.

Then, apply destructive patterns only to executable contexts:

- Executed command words and arguments
- Pipe targets (right side of `|`)
- Command substitution (`$(...)`, backticks)
- Inline interpreter strings (`bash -c`, `python -c`, `node -e`, etc.)
- Heredoc bodies (handled by the heredoc epic)

…and **skip** matching for clearly data-only contexts:

- Option values for known “documentation/metadata” commands (Beads `bd`, git commit messages, etc.)
- Grep/Ripgrep pattern arguments
- Echo/printf arguments

## Goals

1. **Dramatically reduce false positives** for doc/test workflows while preserving true-positive blocks for real execution.
2. **Keep performance high:** the non-risky path should stay microseconds.
3. **Provide explainability hooks:** when a command is allowed due to data-context detection, we should be able to explain why (for debugging).

## Architectural constraints

- This layer runs before pack regex evaluation and before AST/heredoc scanning.
- It must be conservative: if context is ambiguous, treat as executable (or escalate to deeper analysis), but avoid blocking obvious documentation strings.

## Success Criteria

- The examples in “Why this epic exists” are allowed.
- Real executions are still blocked:
  - `bash -c "rm -rf /"`
  - `echo hi | bash -c "rm -rf /"`
  - `python -c "import os; os.system('rm -rf /')"`
- Context classification has regression tests for:
  - quoting (single/double/escaped)
  - pipes
  - command substitution
  - xargs
  - interpreter flags (-c/-e/-p)

## Notes for future self

- If we add a “warn mode” or confidence scoring, this epic provides the context signals.
- Don’t overfit to specific tools; start with a minimal safe-string-arg registry and expand based on real false positives.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-07T19:57:23.162019558-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:51:22.160543876-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-yza","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:53:37.084188042-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.1","title":"Define Safe String-Argument Registry v1 (commands + flags)","description":"## Objective

Create a **versioned** registry of commands whose arguments are predominantly *data*, not code, so we can suppress false positives without compromising safety.

## Background

Many developer workflows involve writing *about* dangerous commands:

- issue trackers (`bd create --description=...`)
- git commit/tag messages (`git commit -m ...`)
- search tools (`rg -e "rm -rf" ...`)
- printing examples (`echo "git reset --hard"`)

Without recognizing these contexts, regex matching will cause velocity-destroying false positives.

## Scope (v1)

Start with a small, high-value registry:

### Always-data commands

- `echo`, `printf`

### Beads CLI

- `bd create`: `--description`, `--title`, `--notes`
- `bd update`: `--description`, `--title`, `--notes`

### Git messages

- `git commit -m`, `git commit --message`
- `git tag -m`, `git tag --message`
- `git notes add -m`

### Search tools

- `grep` pattern args (`-e`, `--regexp`, `-F`, `--fixed-strings`)
- `rg` pattern args (`-e`, `--regexp`, `--fixed-strings`)

### Optional candidates (add only with tests)

- `sed` scripts in `-e` (can be code; treat as code, not data)
- `awk` programs (code; treat as code)

## Design requirements

- Registry must be **explicit and test-driven**.
- Registry must be conservative: only suppress matching where we are confident strings are not executed.
- Provide a way to extend the registry via config in the future (but do not over-engineer).

## Acceptance criteria

- Add unit tests for each registry entry demonstrating at least one previously false-positive case that is now allowed.
- Add at least one counterexample test per category to ensure we do not suppress *real* execution (e.g., `bash -c "rm -rf /"` must still be evaluated).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:02:56.784418941-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T00:40:13.132797997-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T00:40:13.132797997-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented SafeStringRegistry v1 with: all_args_data (echo, printf), flag_data_pairs (git -m, bd --description/--title/--notes/--reason, grep -e/-F, rg -e/--fixed-strings, gh -t/-b/-m, cargo --message, npm --message). Includes SafeFlagEntry with short/both/long constructors and SAFE_STRING_REGISTRY static. 25+ unit tests verify registry behavior. All 98 lib + 92 main + 137 E2E tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.1","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T20:02:56.795312367-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
